events.title: First Gutenberg Bible Printed
events.summary: Johannes Gutenberg completed the first major printed book, the Bible, revolutionizing access to information.
events.details: Using movable type, the Gutenberg Bible was the first major book printed in the West. This innovation sparked a printing revolution, making books and information accessible to a wider audience.

events.title: Von Steuben Begins Training Continental Army
events.summary: Baron von Steuben arrived at Valley Forge to train the struggling Continental Army.
events.details: His rigorous training and drills transformed the ragtag soldiers into a disciplined, professional fighting force. This proved essential for American victory in the Revolutionary War.

events.title: Alamo Defenders Inspire Texas Revolution
events.summary: The heroic defense of the Alamo began, becoming a powerful symbol of courage.
events.details: Though the defenders were ultimately overcome, their sacrifice for Texas liberty inspired others to join the fight. The cry 'Remember the Alamo!' became a rallying point for the revolution.

events.title: Mississippi Readmitted to the United States
events.summary: Mississippi was officially readmitted to the Union following the Civil War.
events.details: This marked a significant step in the Reconstruction era's efforts to reunite the nation. It required the state to adopt a new constitution including protections for African American rights.

events.title: First US State Antitrust Law Enacted
events.summary: Alabama became the first U.S. state to pass an antitrust law, promoting fair competition.
events.details: This pioneering legislation aimed to prevent business monopolies and protect consumers from price-fixing. It set a precedent for future state and federal regulations to promote fair markets.

events.title: Iconic Tootsie Roll Candy Introduced
events.summary: Leo Hirshfield introduced the world to the iconic and beloved Tootsie Roll candy.
events.details: This chewy, chocolatey candy became an American classic, known for its affordability and unique texture. It has remained a popular treat for over a century, bringing simple joy to millions.

events.title: US Regulates Radio for Public Good
events.summary: The U.S. Federal Radio Commission was created to regulate radio use for the public.
events.details: This act brought order to the chaotic airwaves, assigning frequencies to prevent interference. It established the principle that the radio spectrum was a public resource, paving the way for broadcasting.

events.title: Woody Guthrie Writes an American Anthem
events.summary: Folk singer Woody Guthrie wrote the lyrics to his iconic song, 'This Land Is Your Land'.
events.details: Created as a response to 'God Bless America,' Guthrie's song became a beloved anthem of American populism. It celebrates the country's natural beauty and the idea it belongs to all people.

events.title: Plutonium Element First Chemically Identified
events.summary: Dr. Glenn T. Seaborg and his team first produced and isolated the element plutonium.
events.details: This landmark discovery in nuclear chemistry expanded the periodic table and our understanding of physics. It later enabled applications in nuclear energy generation and power for deep space missions.

events.title: Iconic Flag Raising on Iwo Jima
events.summary: U.S. Marines famously raised the American flag atop Mount Suribachi during World War II.
events.details: The moment, captured in an iconic photograph, became a powerful symbol of American perseverance and unity. It boosted morale on the home front and represented hope for eventual victory.

events.title: First Mass Polio Vaccinations Begin
events.summary: The first mass inoculation of children against polio using the Salk vaccine began.
events.details: This event in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, marked a turning point in the fight against the devastating disease. The Salk vaccine's success led to a dramatic decline in polio cases worldwide.

events.title: Artificial Turf Debuts in International Soccer
events.summary: The first international soccer match on artificial turf was played in the Houston Astrodome.
events.details: This game demonstrated the viability of 'AstroTurf' for new kinds of venues. The innovation allowed sports to be played year-round in indoor stadiums, changing venue design forever.

events.title: Supernova 1987A Observed from Earth
events.summary: Astronomers observed Supernova 1987A, the closest such event seen since 1604.
events.details: This spectacular cosmic event provided invaluable data for understanding stellar evolution and the death of massive stars. It was the first supernova that allowed scientists to detect neutrinos, confirming theories.

events.title: Scientists Announce First Cloned Mammal, Dolly
events.summary: Scientists at the Roslin Institute in Scotland announced the successful cloning of a sheep named Dolly.
events.details: Dolly was the first mammal cloned from an adult cell, a monumental scientific breakthrough. This achievement opened new frontiers in genetics, medicine, and biotechnology, sparking global discussion.

events.title: Earth-Like Exoplanet System Discovered
events.summary: NASA announced the discovery of the TRAPPIST-1 system with seven Earth-sized exoplanets.
events.details: This remarkable discovery energized the search for extraterrestrial life, as several planets orbit in the star's habitable zone. It represented a significant step in understanding planetary systems beyond our own.
